name: Conditional Reliability of Toxicity Signals for Multilingual and Code-Mixed Abuse Detection
summary: arXiv:2607.15861v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Moderation systems increasingly rely on external toxicity tools, but those tools are unreliable under code-mixing, transliteration, slang, and lang...
description: arXiv:2607.15861v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Moderation systems increasingly rely on external toxicity tools, but those tools are unreliable under code-mixing, transliteration, slang, and language mismatch. We study the \emph{conditional reliability} of toxicity priors in Indian multilingual and code-mixed short text: English toxicity, Indic abuse, and rule-based severity cues can be usefu...
author: Indraveni Chebolu, Rohan Singh, Arnab Mallick, Harmesh Rana
